pi_items.source: audit_attendhrm_env.md references employee 1042 as existing in demo database; task setup scripts not present but task.json hooks reference C:\workspace\tasks\add_employee_qualification\setup_task.ps1
audit_attendhrm_env.md explicitly states: 'Dexter Riley (1042)' is an IT Manager in the demo database
No references found in demo data, CSV files, or task setup scripts. Appears to be synthetic task data.
No references found in demo data or import files. Appears to be synthetic task data.
No references found in demo data. University of Michigan is a real, well-known institution (founded 1817, located in Ann Arbor, MI), but this specific claim is not verified against task data sources.
No references found in demo data or task setup. Plausible but unverified.
No references found in demo data. The value 3.8 is plausible for a GPA (on 4.0 scale) but not verified.
No references found in demo data. 'Distinction' is a real academic honor (typically >=85% or 3.7+ GPA), but this specific claim is unverified.

privileged_info_summary: Employee ID 1042 exists in the AttendHRM demo database, but the actual employee name is Dexter Riley (verified in audit_attendhrm_env.md), NOT Robert Clarke as claimed in the task metadata. This is a critical contradiction. The qualification details (MBA in Finance from University of Michigan, class of 2018, 3.8 GPA, graduated with distinction) appear to be synthetic data created for this task, as they do not appear in any demo database records, CSV import files, or documentation. The task has no setup_task.ps1 or export_result.ps1 scripts present in the repository, unlike other similar tasks.
